> 📖 Before filing: skim the [Code Review Baseline](docs/architecture/CODE_REVIEW_BASELINE.md).
> It lists the non-negotiable invariants this repo's reviewers (CodeRabbit and humans) check on every PR — architectural layering, Constants vs Defaults vs Assumptions, the Analyst boundary, ADR discipline, etc.
>
> **How review works here:** [CodeRabbit](https://coderabbit.ai/) (configured via `.coderabbit.yaml`) auto-reviews PRs and posts advisory comments — its comments do **not** block merges. Merge gating is enforced by [`.github/CODEOWNERS`](.github/CODEOWNERS); the listed owners must approve before merge.

<!--
The sections below are optional but make review faster. Delete any that don't apply.
-->

## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences: what does this PR do and why? -->

## Scope of change

<!--
Which layers / areas does this touch? (client / server / engine / calc / shared,
docs, infra, tests, etc.) Anything explicitly *out* of scope?
-->

## Screenshots / UI evidence

<!--
If this changes anything visible, drop before/after screenshots or a short
screen recording. Skip this section for backend-only or docs-only PRs.
-->

## Test / verification evidence

<!--
How did you verify this works? Examples:
- `npm run check` passes
- `npm test -- <pattern>` passes (paste the relevant output)
- `npm run verify` opinion stayed UNQUALIFIED
- Manually exercised <flow> in the running app
-->

## Reviewer notes

<!--
Anything reviewers should look at first, known follow-ups, or questions you
want a second opinion on.
-->
